Uranium enrichment not part of civil nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia, US energy secretary says
data.description:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) -Uranium enrichment is not part of the deal on civil nuclear energy reached with Saudi Arabia, U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright said on Wednesday.

"It is about civilian, civil use of nuclear power. It's not about enrichment. It's not about anything related to weapons. It's just about generating electricity, secure, reliable, affordable electricity," Wright said in an interview with Fox News.

The agreement is about construction of a large nuclear power plant in Saudi Arabia, he said. It is one of several agreements announced during Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman's visit to Washington.

(Reporting by Katharine Jackson; Editing by Doina Chiacu)

Leonardo and EDGE to launch defence joint venture in UAE in 2026
data.description:
MILAN (Reuters) -Italian defence and aerospace company Leonardo and Abu Dhabi's EDGE group will launch a joint venture in the United Arab Emirates next year to design, develop and produce defence systems, the companies said at the Dubai Airshow on Wednesday.

The venture will work on systems including sensors, platforms and integration solutions, for the United Arab Emirates (UAE) and selected export markets based on products already offered by Leonardo, the companies said.

The joint venture, which was first outlined in a memorandum of understanding signed earlier this year, will be 51% owned by EDGE and 49% by Leonardo.

Leonardo CEO Roberto Cingolani said the deal arrived after "months of intense work between the partners".

"By collaborating across several critical domains including air, land, sea, and electro-optics, we can tailor solutions ... through the UAE and new untapped markets," EDGE Managing Director and CEO Hamad Al Marar said in the statement.

EDGE, which is owned by the Abu Dhabi government, is seeking to broaden development and production partnerships, particularly with U.S. defence giants, Al Marar said in an interview this week.

The joint venture will also be involved in testing, industrialisation, and training, as well as building a skilled local workforce, the groups said.

(Reporting by Cristina Carlevaro; Editing by Giulia Segreti and Conor Humphries)

South Korean ferry with 267 on board runs aground, rescue underway
data.description:
SEOUL (Reuters) -A South Korean passenger ferry carrying 267 passengers and crew ran aground on Wednesday off the southwestern tip of the Korean peninsula, triggering a rescue operation and stirring memories of a major ferry disaster in 2014.

President Lee Jae Myung, who is currently travelling in the Middle East, ordered a swift rescue of all on board to prevent casualties and for details of the operation to be made public as they occur, the Yonhap News Agency reported.

Authorities were deploying all possible resources in the rescue operation, the coast guard said in a statement.

It said it believed there were no casualties so far from the incident and the boat was not taking on water.

RESCUE BOATS DEPLOYED

The 26,000-tonne ferry, the Queen Jenuvia 2, was travelling from Jeju Island to Mokpo, according to the coast guard. The boat's Mokpo-based operator, Seaworld Ferry, lists the vessel as having a capacity of 1,010 passengers, as well as multiple lower decks for large vehicles and passenger cars.

A coast guard official said the ferry had struck a rocky island near Jindo.

Video footage showed passengers wearing life vests awaiting transfer to rescue boats, which were approaching the ferry. The vessel's bow appeared stuck on the edge of a small island, but it appeared to be upright and the passengers appeared calm.

Weather conditions at the scene were reported to be fair with light winds.

Prime Minister Kim Min-seok has ordered all available vessels and equipment to be mobilised to rescue passengers, his office said.

PREVIOUS SINKING WAS ONE OF COUNTRY'S WORST DISASTERS

Memories are still fresh in South Korea of the sinking of the Sewol ferry in 2014, one of the country's worst disasters in which more than 300 people died, mostly school children heading for a school trip to Jeju.

The Sewol was sailing from the west coast port of Incheon when it sank near the site of Wednesday's incident, though further off the island of Jindo.

The ferry, which was later found to have been overloaded with cargo and illegally modified to carry more than designed, went into a turn too fast and began listing.

It then lay on its side as passengers awaited rescue, which was slow to come, before sinking as the country watched on live television.

Many of the victims were found in their cabins, where they had been told to wait by crew while the captain and some crew members were taken aboard the first coast guard vessels to arrive at the scene.

(Reporting by Heekyong Yang, Ju-min Park, Joyce Lee and Jack Kim; Editing by Ed Davies, Aidan Lewis)

By Heekyong Yang and Ju-min Park

data.title: Financial 15 Split Corp. monthly dividend declaration for Class A & preferred share
Nokia Shares Plunge Following Disappointment Over New Financial Targets
data.description:
Nokia's stock experienced a sharp decline on Wednesday, marking its fifth consecutive day of losses. As of 3:45 p.m. local time, the share price had dropped by 7.0 percent in trading on the Helsinki stock exchange.

Several analysts have voiced their disappointment with the Finnish telecom giant's newly announced long-term financial targets, revised strategy, and organizational restructuring, all unveiled during the company's capital markets day, according to Bloomberg News.

Analysts at Barclays described the financial targets set for 2028 as disappointing. Meanwhile, Citi acknowledged Nokia's investment in AI infrastructure as a positive step, but noted that this move had already been anticipated by the market. Citi maintains a sell recommendation on the stock, with a target price of EUR3.90.

US Census Bureau to release Sept retail sales, durable goods orders reports next week
data.description:
(Reuters) -The U.S. Commerce Department's Census Bureau said on Wednesday it would publish both September retail sales and food services and durable goods orders reports next week. Both were delayed by the government shutdown.

The monthly sales report for retail and food services will be issued on Tuesday, while the durable goods orders report is set for publication on Wednesday. The reports were initially due in October.

The 43-day shutdown, the longest in history, halted the collection, processing and publishing of government-issued economic data, including the closely watched monthly employment and inflation reports.

(Reporting by Bhargav Acharya in Toronto; Editing by David Ljunggren)

data.title: TSX rises on commodity boost as investors await Nvidia results
data.description:
(Reuters) -Canada's main stock index edged higher on Wednesday, supported by higher gold prices, while investor attention centered on Nvidia's quarterly earnings due after market close.

At 9:30 a.m. ET, Toronto's S&P/TSX composite index was up 0.29% at 30,122.36 points.

(Reporting by Avinash P in Bengaluru; Editing by Shreya Biswas)

Indian state regulator delays power deal with Adani's $2 billion project over cost issues
data.description:
NEW DELHI (Reuters) -The power regulator for northern Indian state Uttar Pradesh has delayed approval of a power supply deal with Adani Group's new $2 billion coal power project over lack of clarity around costs, a filing showed on Wednesday, six months after the announcement.

Adani Power in May won a contract to supply 1,500 megawatts of power from a coal plant in Uttar Pradesh at 5.38 rupees ($0.0638) per unit.

The lack of clarity over costs stems from a decision by the Indian government in July to ease rules for some coal plants from installing equipment that would remove sulfur dioxide from exhausts while burning coal.

The easing of rules is expected to bring in billions of rupees in savings for the coal plant operators.

The state power regulator noted in the order that the power utility, Uttar Pradesh Power Corporation, failed to provide its own analysis of savings or cost changes from not installing the equipment.

The commission directed the state power utility to make Adani Power a party to the case and submit detailed cost assessments within two weeks. The case is now listed for hearing on December 18.

The regulator noted in its previous hearing in September that once the equipment was confirmed as not required, the power utility should have approached the commission with revised fixed charges and operating expenses reflecting savings for the state.

It also said the utility should have assessed the impact of revised goods and services tax rates on coal under the power supply agreement.

Indian state electricity distributors are signing long-term contracts with coal-fired power generators to meet a projected surge in evening demand, despite the country's efforts to expand clean energy capacity.

(Reporting by Sethuraman NR; Editing by Shreya Biswas)

By Sethuraman N R

Defense Stocks Weaken Amid Possible U.S. Ukraine Initiative
data.description:
FRANKFURT (dpa-AFX) - Shares of Rheinmetall fell sharply on Wednesday, at one point dropping to their lowest level since late August. The Düsseldorf-based defense contractor's stock lost nearly eight percent at its lowest, reaching EUR1,581. Subsequently, the shares hovered around the 200-day moving average, a key indicator of long-term trends. Shares of tank transmission manufacturer Renk and radar specialist Hensoldt also declined, albeit less steeply, but overall displayed similar chart instability.

The selling pressure was triggered by a possible new U.S. initiative aimed at ending the war in Ukraine. According to the U.S. news site "Axios," U.S. Special Envoy Steve Witkoff is reportedly holding separate talks with both Russian and Ukrainian counterparts, as reported overnight.

Later in the afternoon, U.S. broadcaster "ABC" reported that senior American military officials had arrived in Ukraine for peace talks and might soon meet with Russian officials as well.

Recently, however, defense stocks managed to pare back some of their losses. Efforts like these are unlikely to significantly alter the threat landscape or the long-term prospects for restoring European defense capabilities. More decisive for the sector remain the growth targets set by the companies themselves--targets that Rheinmetall outlined for 2030 during its recent capital markets day.

Analysts responded by raising their price targets: Experts at Morgan Stanley set a new target of EUR2,500, while Benjamin Heelan at Bank of America (BofA) went as high as EUR2,540. Heelan wrote that Rheinmetall's ambitious medium-term goals are well above consensus estimates and are likely to push those estimates higher.

Investors, however, have become accustomed to positive surprises, commented Adrien Rabier of Bernstein Research, which may explain the muted share price reaction to the capital markets day. The medium-term targets were largely in line with expectations. Nevertheless, Rheinmetall shares had reached their highest level in four weeks just a day earlier.

According to the dpa news agency, EU sources said on Wednesday that while talks between the U.S. and both warring parties were known, the new plan had not yet been seen. Currently, it appears that Russia may have an interest in spreading such reports as a form of renewed distraction, especially as pressure mounts from new U.S. sanctions against the country's oil industry.

data.title: Platinum Market Forecast to Recover With Small Supply Surplus Expected in 2026 -- Commodities Roundup
data.description:
MARKET MOVEMENTS:

--Brent crude oil is down 2.5% to $63.23 a barrel.

--European benchmark gas is down 3% to 30.75 euros a megawatt-hour.

--Gold futures are up 1.1% to $4,113.20 a troy ounce.

--LME three-month copper futures are up 0.8% at $10,782 a metric ton


TOP STORY:

Platinum Market Forecast to Recover With Small Supply Surplus Expected in 2026

The platinum market is showing signs of recovery after three years of deficit, with a leading industry body now forecasting a shift toward a modest surplus next year as long as global trade tensions subside.

The World Platinum Investment Council expects a more balanced market in 2026, with a modest 20,000-ounce surplus. This turnaround is largely driven by a sharp decline in investment demand due to expected outflows from CME warehouses and higher prices prompting profit-taking among exchange-traded fund holders.

"Previously, we were focusing on individual components of the underlying market, factors like jewelry," said Edward Sterck, research director at the WPIC. "But we've got this situation now where the macroeconomic and geopolitical overlay is the main thing."


OTHER STORIES:

ExxonMobil to Close Scottish Chemicals Plant

ExxonMobil plans to close its Fife ethylene plant in Scotland next year, a move that affects more than 400 jobs.

The U.S. energy major said it had considered various options to continue operations, including looking for a potential buyer, but concluded that the plant isn't currently commercially viable.

--

Ithaca Energy Earnings, Production Rise on Eni's U.K. Assets Merger

Ithaca Energy said the integration of Eni's U.K. assets fueled earnings and production growth over the first nine months of the year.

The London-listed energy company said pretax profit for the period ended Sept. 30 rose to $668.1 million from $183.7 million in the same period a year prior.


MARKET TALKS:

Oil Slides Ahead of Official U.S. Crude Inventories Data -- Market Talk

1357 GMT - Oil prices drop as concerns over U.S. crude and gasoline inventories outweigh fears of disruptions to Russian supplies. Brent crude is down 2.7% to $63.15 a barrel, while WTI slides 2.8% to $58.98 a barrel. "The official EIA inventory report will give much more heft to the state of U.S. demand later," analysts at PVM Insights say. However, reports of a significant crude build are once again pointing to "demand destruction in the U.S. and a world heading toward oversupply in 2026." Oil's decline comes despite a tighter fuel market due to Ukrainian attacks against Russian energy infrastructure and fears that U.S. sanctions against major Russian producers could disrupt global supplies. Traders now await Thursday's U.S. jobs data, which could shift expectations for future Fed rate cuts. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

U.S. Natural Gas Futures Rise on Weather Outlook -- Market Talk

0852 ET - U.S. natural gas futures are higher with the market focusing on even small shifts in the weather outlook. Overnight forecasts turned colder for the end of November and expectations remain for strong December heating demand. While warmer-than-usual near-term weather helps cap gains, "traders are more concerned with developments beyond the end of November as winter risk ramps up," Andy Huenefeld of Pinebrook Energy Advisors says in a note. "For now, the setup for December looks very supportive of elevated heating needs." Nymex natural gas is up 3.2% at $4.511/mmBtu.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

--

Gold Rises With Focus on Fed Minutes, U.S. Labor Data -- Market Talk

1331 GMT - Gold prices continue to climb on safe-haven demand, with investors now awaiting the Federal Reserve's minutes later on Wednesday and key U.S. jobs data. Futures in New York rise 1% to $4,108.40 a troy ounce, while spot gold is up 0.5% to $4,067.43 an ounce. The Fed's minutes are expected to provide insight into policymakers' views on the future path of interest rates during their most recent meeting. Meanwhile, a stronger-than-expected nonfarm payrolls print on Thursday could reignite speculation about further cuts in December, benefiting non-yielding bullion. However, this week's data only covers September due to the government shutdown backlog, offering limited insight into the current economic landscape. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Palm Oil Prices End Higher, Tracking Strength in Soybean Oil Market -- Market Talk

1010 GMT - Palm oil closed higher, tracking strength in the soybean oil market, says David Ng, a trader at Kuala Lumpur-based Iceberg X. Expectations on demand remains weak due to seasonality, which weighs on upward price momentum, he adds. Ng sees support for CPO futures at 4,150 ringgit a ton and resistance at 4,300 ringgit a ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for January delivery closed 17 ringgit higher at 4,226 ringgit a ton. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

--

Copper Inches Higher But Fed Uncertainty, Easing Supply Concerns Weigh On Sentiment -- Market Talk

0917 GMT - Copper prices edge higher, though investors remain cautious ahead of key U.S. data that could provide insights into the Federal Reserve's next policy move. "Several policymakers have cautioned against a further reduction in rates for now, hurting the outlook for demand for commodities," ANZ analysts say. Recent price gains have been supported by concerns over supply disruptions in key regions. However, Freeport-McMoRan's plans to resume large-scale production at its Grasberg mine in Indonesia by the second quarter of next year has eased those fears, weighing on market sentiment. Copper futures on the London Metal Exchange are up 0.2% to $10,717.50 a metric ton. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Russian Supply Fears Reflected in Middle Distillate Market, ING Says -- Market Talk

0901 GMT - Concerns over the impact of U.S. sanctions and Ukrainian attacks on Russian oil supplies are well-reflected in the middle distillate market, ING analysts say. The ICE gasoil crack--the difference between the price of crude and the price of refined products like diesel--rose above $38 a barrel from around $23 a barrel in mid-October, signaling that the profit margin for refining crude oil has significantly increased. Meanwhile, the prompt ICE gasoil timspread--the price difference between the near-term and longer-term delivery contracts--has moved into a backwardation of more than $43 a ton, indicating that traders expect current supplies of gasoil to be particularly tight. "Worries over Russian diesel supply amid sanctions and Ukrainian attacks on Russian refineries are driving the market's strength," ING analysts say. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Oil Falls as Weaker Demand Signals Amplify Oversupply Fears -- Market Talk

0846 GMT - Oil prices fall in early trade on reports of rising U.S. crude inventories, though further losses are capped by concerns over Russian flows. Brent crude and WTI are both down 1% to $64.28 and $60.09 a barrel, respectively. "Despite some Asian buyers pausing Russian purchases and stronger European diesel markets, broader fundamentals remain bearish," Soojin Kim from MUFG says. Crude stocks rose by around 4.45 million barrels last week, according to reports citing figures from the American Petroleum Institute ahead of official EIA data. The build signals weaker demand in the world's largest oil consumer, fueling fears of an oversupplied global market. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Gold Rises on Safe-Haven Demand After Market Rout -- Market Talk

0831 GMT - Gold prices regain ground in early trading on renewed safe-haven demand, despite diminishing prospects of a December interest-rate cut by the Federal Reserve. New York futures rise 0.5% to $4,086.10 a troy ounce, while spot gold is up 0.5% to $4,067.43 an ounce. "Equity markets were in the red across the globe amid jitters over lofty valuations," ANZ Research analysts say. "The subsequent risk-off mood saw some buying of haven assets such as gold." Traders remain cautious ahead of key U.S. economic data, which were delayed by the government shutdown backlog, with all eyes on Thursday's nonfarm payrolls report. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Gold Stocks Rise as Safe-Haven Demand Returns -- Market Talk

0825 GMT - Gold stocks rise in opening trade as gold prices tick up. Futures in New York are up 0.55% at $4,089.80 a troy ounce after sliding in previous sessions as traders saw the chances of a cut to the U.S. Federal Reserve rate fall. Higher interest rates weigh on non-yielding assets like gold. However, safe-haven demand now has pushed prices higher as investors worry about lofty valuations of technology stocks, ANZ analysts write. In London, gold and silver miner Fresnillo is up 3%, Hochschild Mining rises 2.5% and Endeavour Mining is up 2.15%. In Johannesburg, Sibanye-Stillwater rises 1.9% and while Harmony Gold rises 0.8%. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

--

Iron Ore Rises; Fundamentals Point to Supply-Demand Imbalance -- Market Talk

0245 GMT - Iron ore rises in Asian trading, with the most-traded iron-ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange 0.8% higher at 791.50 yuan a ton. Prices have continued their upward trend, indicating strong buying pressure, Nanhua Futures analysts say in commentary. It adds that the market's fundamentals signal a supply-demand imbalance, and inventories continue to accumulate, though there is a structural shortage in terms of deliverable products. Shipments have picked up in the short term, with smaller miners keeping volumes high, Nanhua adds. (jason.chau@wsj.com)


Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-19-25 0929ET
